craft 0.0.0.9000

  • Reference

Generate a CRAFT reproducibility report

Source: R/report_craft.R
report.Rd

Renders an Rmd template that records the full CRAFT run: which LLM, which version, which prompt(s), all metrics, thresholds, audit decisions, and any DSL correction applied. Scholars include this report alongside their paper as a methods supplement so the LLM workflow is reproducible.

Usage

report(
  role_task,
  llm,
  version,
  prompt_path,
  metrics = NULL,
  audit = NULL,
  dsl = NULL,
  thresholds = NULL,
  output = "craft_report.html",
  format = NULL,
  template = NULL
)

Arguments

role_task

A craft_role object from role.

llm

Character: model name (e.g. "GPT-5").

version

Character: exact model identifier (e.g. "gpt-5-2025-09-15").

prompt_path

Path to a text file containing the prompt(s) used in the study. The file contents are pasted verbatim into the report.

metrics

A list with elements reliability and validity (from dual) or a stab result.

audit

Optional data frame from audit. If supplied, the report shows 1-2 example rows with text and rationales to illustrate the audit step.

dsl

Optional data frame from dsl_cmp.

thresholds

Named list of threshold decisions (e.g. list(confidence = 0.55, low = 0.31)).

output

Path to write the rendered report. If relative, resolved against getwd(). Default "craft_report.html" writes to the current working directory. Use "craft_report.pdf" for PDF.

format

Optional explicit format: "html", "pdf", or "both". If NULL (default), inferred from the output extension. When "both", writes both an HTML and a PDF file alongside each other.

template

Optional alternative Rmd template. Default uses the one shipped in inst/rmd/report_template.Rmd.

Value

Invisibly returns the absolute path(s) to the rendered report(s).

Details

The report is written as **prose** (not raw R output) so it can be submitted as a methods-transparency supplement. Format is detected from the output file extension: .html for HTML output, .pdf for PDF (requires a LaTeX install, e.g. via tinytex::install_tinytex()). The default writes to the user's current working directory so the file is easy to find.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  report(
    role_task   = rt,
    llm         = "GPT-5",
    version     = "gpt-5-2025-09-15",
    prompt_path = "prompts/dev4.txt",
    metrics     = dt,
    audit       = a,
    dsl         = dsl_results,
    thresholds  = list(confidence = 0.55, low = 0.31),
    output      = "my_craft_report.pdf"   # writes to getwd()
  )

  # Both html and pdf in one call:
  report(..., output = "my_craft_report", format = "both")
} # }
